facts.text: Kendall was named for Henry John Broughton Kendall, a company director who moved to the area in the 1900s to manage the company’s land.
The first public school in Kendall opened in 1929, originally called Kendall School and now known as Kenwood K‑8 Center.
In 1883 the Florida Land and Mortgage Company bought the land from the state, but because it wasn’t open to homesteading, development stayed slow into the 20th century.
Two Seminole camps once dotted the Kendall area, and the Seminole people continued to live there into the 1940s.
Kendall sits just 14 miles southwest of downtown Miami, making it a close suburban neighbor to the city’s core.
Local radio and television stations in Kendall are part of the larger Miami market, giving residents access to the city’s media outlets.
